linkedTasks: Develop a group of products or accessories, and market them through venues such as boutiques or mail-order catalogs.
Market bank products to individuals and firms, promoting bank services that may meet customers' needs.
Market products, services, or events.

proposedNodes.title: Market Products
sourceTasks: Develop a group of products or accessories, and market them through venues such as boutiques or mail-order catalogs.
Market products, services, or events.
proposedNodes.reason: Sources 1 and 3 support marketing products as a generic product activity after other linked development, accessory-selling, event, and service-selling activities are accounted for.

proposedNodes.title: Market Bank Products
sourceTasks: Market bank products to individuals and firms, promoting bank services that may meet customers' needs.
proposedNodes.reason: Source 2 specifically supports marketing bank products, which is a restricted product-marketing activity.

proposedNodes.title: Market Services
sourceTasks: Market products, services, or events.
proposedNodes.reason: Source 3 separately supports marketing services, and the existing Sell Services node does not cover the market-verb activity.

judgments.agentReasoning: The residual evidence combines generic product marketing, restricted bank-product marketing, and service marketing, which are separable activity restrictions under the same leading verb.

linkedTasks: Sell alcoholic beverages to passengers.
Sell food, beverages, or tobacco to players.

proposedNodes.title: Sell Alcoholic Beverages
sourceTasks: Sell alcoholic beverages to passengers.
proposedNodes.reason: Task 1 specifically restricts the sold object to alcoholic beverages.

proposedNodes.title: Sell Beverages
sourceTasks: Sell food, beverages, or tobacco to players.
proposedNodes.reason: After existing food and tobacco sibling nodes are accounted for, task 2 still supports selling generic beverages.

judgments.agentReasoning: The current node combines a restricted beverage activity with a generic beverage activity, while food and tobacco from task 2 are already covered by existing nodes.

proposedNodes.title: Sell Hair Care Products
sourceTasks: Demonstrate and sell hair care products and cosmetics.
proposedNodes.reason: The task specifically supports selling hair care products after the demonstration activity is already covered.

proposedNodes.title: Sell Cosmetics
sourceTasks: Demonstrate and sell hair care products and cosmetics.
proposedNodes.reason: Cosmetics are a distinct sold product category in the source task.

proposedNodes.title: Sell Financial Products
sourceTasks: Explain, promote, or sell products or services, such as travelers' checks, savings bonds, money orders, and cashier's checks, using computerized information about customers to tailor recommendations.
proposedNodes.reason: The examples are financial instruments or banking products, while explaining, promoting, and services are already covered by siblings.

proposedNodes.title: Sell Nail Care Products
sourceTasks: Promote and sell nail care products.
proposedNodes.reason: The task specifically restricts the sold product to nail care products after promotion is already covered.

proposedNodes.title: Sell Agricultural Products
sourceTasks: Sell agricultural products.
proposedNodes.reason: The task explicitly restricts the sold product to agricultural products.

proposedNodes.title: Sell Postal Products
sourceTasks: Sell and collect payment for products such as stamps, prepaid mail envelopes, and money orders.
Sell mail products, and accept payment for products and mailing charges.
proposedNodes.reason: Stamps, prepaid mail envelopes, money orders, and mail products form a coherent postal-products restriction after payment activities are already covered.

proposedNodes.title: Sell Funeral Products
sourceTasks: Sell funeral services, products, or merchandise to clients.
proposedNodes.reason: The residual product evidence is restricted to funeral-related products after services and merchandise are already covered.

proposedNodes.title: Sell Products
sourceTasks: Sell products being promoted and keep records of sales.
Sell products or services.
Sell products or services.
Sell products, services, or memberships.
proposedNodes.reason: These tasks retain an unmodified generic product-selling activity after sibling nodes cover services, memberships, records, or payments.

proposedNodes.title: Sell Technical Products
sourceTasks: Sell products requiring extensive technical expertise and support for installation and use, such as material handling equipment, numerical-control machinery, or computer systems.
Sell technical and scientific products that are environmentally sound or designed for environmental remediation.
proposedNodes.reason: Both tasks restrict the sold products to technical or scientific products requiring specialized expertise or environmental application.

judgments.agentReasoning: The residual evidence mixes generic product selling with several coherent restricted product categories, so one broad node would obscure distinct activity senses.

linkedTasks: Recommend and sell lotions, tonics, or other cosmetic supplies.

Sell pet food and supplies.

proposedNodes.title: Sell Cosmetic Supplies
sourceTasks: Recommend and sell lotions, tonics, or other cosmetic supplies.
proposedNodes.reason: After lotion and tonic sales are covered by existing nodes, the residual evidence is selling other cosmetic supplies.

proposedNodes.title: Sell Expedition Supplies
sourceTasks: Sell or rent equipment, clothing, and supplies related to expeditions.
proposedNodes.reason: Equipment and clothing are already covered, leaving supplies related to expeditions.

proposedNodes.title: Sell Pet Supplies
sourceTasks: Sell pet food and supplies.
Sell pet food or supplies to customers.
proposedNodes.reason: Pet food is already covered, leaving the distinct activity of selling pet supplies.

proposedNodes.title: Sell Agricultural Supplies
sourceTasks: Sell supplies, such as seed, feed, fertilizers, or insecticides, arranging for loans or financing as necessary.
proposedNodes.reason: The examples of seed, feed, fertilizers, and insecticides support an agricultural-supplies restriction.

judgments.agentReasoning: The current node combines several restricted supply-selling activities that are separable after accounting for existing sibling nodes.
